svg.aria-label: Mayo Clinic Scheduling Assistant — user journey and architecture poster
svg.title: Mayo Clinic Scheduling Assistant — In Action
svg.text: Mayo Clinic Scheduling Assistant — In Action
From a tap to a tailored, grounded answer · architecture in three layers

g.text: Request, prepare, or change a visit
Web Speech API · es-US locale · live transcription
Mayo Clinic has three U.S. campuses
KB-only · cites every assertion · 3 phone CTAs
The page reacts — not a link out
Most patients can request online or by phone.
Widget dismissed · element pulses · return in place
Single HTML file. No KB. No prompt assembly. Smaller payload.
api/chat.js — gates, builds the system prompt server-side, streams SSE through. Owns kb.json.
Claude Sonnet 4.6 — system prompt assembled server-side, SSE stream out.
Single HTML file, one serverless function,
zero npm dependencies. Drag four files into
Vercel and the demo is live.
Per-IP rate limit, no shared session state,
swap an in-memory bucket for Upstash and go.
KB lives as portable JSON, never ships to the
browser. Every assertion is paired with a
kb-ID. No PHI, no booking, citations enforced.

svg.text: Mayo Clinic Scheduling Assistant · Reference architecture · 2026
v2 · KB externalized · server-built prompt · Claude Sonnet 4.6
